note: High reported turnover should be cross-checked against executable depth and slippage before treating it as real liquidity
Repeated or narrow trade-size bands can indicate mechanical activity and should be compared with peer venues
Regular timing without plausible external demand is a weak anomaly signal that becomes stronger alongside size and VWAP divergence
Persistent reported flow that does not move VWAP or price as expected should be treated as a market-health investigation trigger
One venue showing materially different trade-size distribution from peers can indicate venue-specific incentives or artificial turnover
Wash-trading claims should require multiple agreeing anomaly screens plus documented alternative explanations before escalation
